dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4794 IS: Precision Agriculture Cybersecurity Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Sheehy (for himself and Mr. Schiff) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Homeland Security to submit to Congress a report regarding cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Precision Agriculture Cybersecurity Act.

paragraph.text: The term foreign country of concern means—

subparagraph.text: the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea;

paragraph.text: The term precision agriculture technology means—

subparagraph.text: instrumentation and techniques ranging from sophisticated sensors and software systems to manual sampling and data collection tools that measure, record, and manage spatial and temporal data;
technologies for searching out and assembling information necessary for sound agricultural production decisionmaking;
open systems technologies for data networking and processing that produce valued systems for farm management decisionmaking; and
machines that deliver information-based management practices.

section.header: Report on cybersecurity in precision agriculture technology

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Homeland Security, acting through the Director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, in coordination with the Food and Agriculture Sector Coordinating Council and the Food and Agriculture-Information Sharing and Analysis Center, shall prepare a report containing an assessment of current cyber-threats to precision agriculture technologies.
The report prepared under subsection (a) shall contain—

paragraph.text: an evaluation of all cyber-threats to precision agriculture technologies;
an assessment of cyber-threats to precision agriculture technologies from foreign countries of concern;

subparagraph.text: an identification of gaps in the United States planning for cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies; and

paragraph.text: a review of existing Department of Agriculture and Department of Homeland Security funding opportunities related to enhancing cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies; and
a review of existing staff at the Department of Agriculture and the Department of Homeland Security that contribute to cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies.

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Homeland Security, acting through the Director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, shall make publicly available the report prepared under subsection (a) and submit such report to—

paragraph.text: the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate;
the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives;
the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate;
the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives;
the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate; and
the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives.
The report made publicly available in accordance with subsection (c) shall not contain any classified information.
The report submitted to the congressional committees in accordance with subsection (c) may include a classified annex, if warranted.

section.header: GAO report on coordination and collaboration on cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies across the Federal Government

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study on the coordination and collaboration on cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies across the Federal Government.
The study conducted under subsection (a) shall—

subparagraph.text: assess current coordination and collaboration across the Federal Government on cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies; and
develop recommendations to enhance such collaboration and coordination; and

evaluate the work of the Food and Agriculture Sector Government Coordinating Council (referred to in this paragraph as the GCC) and the Food and Agriculture Sector Coordinating Council (referred to in this paragraph as the SCC) on addressing cybersecurity in precision agriculture technologies; and
identify opportunities to strengthen coordination between the GCC and the SCC.

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General of the United States shall make publicly available a report containing the findings of the study conducted under subsection (a) and submit such report to—

paragraph.text: the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate;
the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives;
the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate; and
the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives.
The report made publicly available in accordance with subsection (c) shall not contain any classified information.
The report submitted to the congressional committees in accordance with subsection (c) may include a classified annex, if warranted.
